description: F56 create+delete block rule lifecycle (Sprint 41 F97, round 2 fix 2026-06-14). Creates a Top-Level-Domain block rule for TLD 'museum' (IANA-registered, verified absent from dev DB at authoring time), confirms the save dialog, then uses the Manage Rules search box to locate and delete the created rule in a teardown testCase -- net DB change is zero. TEST DATA: 'museum' chosen because 'xyz' (prior candidate) was already in the dev DB as source_domain='*.xyz'. 'museum' is in kIanaTlds (iana_tlds.dart) and had count=0 in the top_level_domain rules at time of authoring. Generated pattern: @.*\.museum$ , source_domain: *.museum. FIX 1 -- RADIO SELECTOR: ww_click on type=RadioButton[name*='Top-Level Domain'] returned success but did NOT select the radio (form stayed on Entire Domain default). Fix: click the PARENT GROUP instead -- type=Group[name*='Top-Level Domain']. Verified live: after clicking the Group, SelectionItemPattern IsSelected=True on the RadioButton. FIX 2 -- INPUT FIELD NAME IS MODE-DEPENDENT: in Top-Level-Domain mode the input is type=Edit[name*='Enter TLD'] (full name 'Enter TLD (e.g., .cc, .xyz, .store)'). In Entire-Domain mode (default) the input is type=Edit[name='Enter email, domain, or URL']. The prior script used the Entire-Domain field name in TLD mode, so input was silently ignored. FIX 3 -- SAVE BUTTON OFF-SCREEN + INVOKE->CLICK: ww_window_state maximize added as first step. Verified live: after maximize, Save Rule is IsOffscreen=False at y=734 (window 1020px+ tall), so no ww_scroll step is needed. ww_click (not ww_invoke) used for Save Rule. SCROLL FALLBACK: ww_scroll mode=into_view was initially included but caused a race condition -- the scroll repositioned the Flutter scroll container just before ww_click Save Rule, and the confirm dialog did not appear. Removed. Maximize alone is sufficient on this machine (1920x1080). CONFIRMATION DIALOG: 'Confirm Block Rule' Alert with Button[name='Save'] -- verified live via ww_dump_tree immediately after Save Rule click. TEARDOWN: search '*.museum', click Button[name*='View rule details'], Delete x2 (first Delete opens 'Delete Rule' confirm, second confirms), Back x2 to home -- all selectors verified live.

testCases.title: Create TLD block rule for museum
Delete the museum block rule (teardown -- restores DB state)
